title: Update module github.com/opencontainers/runc to v1.3.1
body:
This PR contains the following updates:

| Package | Change | Age | Confidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| [github.com/opencontainers/runc](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc) | `v1.3.0` -> `v1.3.1` | [![age](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/age/go/github.com%2fopencontainers%2frunc/v1.3.1?slim=true)](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [![confidence](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/confidence/go/github.com%2fopencontainers%2frunc/v1.3.0/v1.3.1?slim=true)](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) |

---

### Release Notes

<details>
<summary>opencontainers/runc (github.com/opencontainers/runc)</summary>

### [`v1.3.1`](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/releases/tag/v1.3.1): runc v1.3.1 -- "この瓦礫の山でよぉ"

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/compare/v1.3.0...v1.3.1)

This is the first patch release of the 1.3.z release series of runc. It
primarily includes some minor fixes for issues found in 1.3.0.

##### Fixed

- Container processes will no longer inherit the CPU affinity of runc by
  default. Instead, the default CPU affinity of container processes will be
  the largest set of CPUs permitted by the container's cpuset cgroup and any
  other system restrictions (such as isolated CPUs). ([#​4041](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4041), [#​4815](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4815), [#​4858](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4858))
- Setting `linux.rootfsPropagation` to `shared` or `unbindable` now functions
  properly. ([#​1755](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/1755), [#​1815](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/1815), [#​4724](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4724), [#​4789](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4789))
- Close seccomp agent connection to prevent resource leaks. ([#​4796](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4796), [#​4799](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4799))
- `runc delete` and `runc stop` can now correctly handle cases where
  `runc create` was killed during setup. Previously it was possible for the
  container to be in such a state that neither `runc stop` nor `runc delete`
  would be unable to kill or delete the container. ([#​4534](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4534), [#​4645](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4645), [#​4757](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4757),
  [#​4788](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4788))
- `runc update` will no longer clear intelRdt state information. ([#​4828](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4828),
  [#​4833](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4833))
- CI: Fix exclusion rules and allow us to run jobs manually. ([#​4760](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4760), [#​4763](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4763))

##### Changed

- Improvements to the deprecation warnings as part of the
  `github.com/opencontainers/cgroups` split. ([#​4784](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4784), [#​4788](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4788))
- Disable the dmem controller in our cgroup tests, as systemd does not yet
  support it. ([#​4806](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4806), [#​4811](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4811))
- `/proc/net/dev` is no longer included in the permitted procfs overmount
  list. Its inclusion was almost certainly an error, and because `/proc/net`
  is a symlink to `/proc/self/net`, overmounting this was almost certainly
  never useful (and will be blocked by future kernel versions). ([#​4817](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4817), [#​4820](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4820))
- Simplify the `prepareCriuRestoreMounts` logic for checkpoint-restore.
  ([#​4765](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4765), [#​4871](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4871))
- CI: Bump `golangci-lint` to v2.1. ([#​4747](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4747), [#​4754](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4754))
- CI: Switch to GitHub-hosted ARM runners. Thanks again to [@​alexellis](https://redirect.github.com/alexellis) for
  supporting runc's ARM CI up until now. ([#​4844](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4844), [#​4856](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4856), [#​4867](https://redirect.github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4867))

##### Static Linking Notices

The `runc` binary distributed with this release are *statically linked* with
the following [GNU LGPL-2.1][lgpl-2.1] licensed libraries, with `runc` acting
as a "work that uses the Library":

[lgpl-2.1]: https://www.gnu.org/licenses/old-licenses/lgpl-2.1.en.html

- [libseccomp](https://redirect.github.com/seccomp/libseccomp)

The versions of these libraries were not modified from their upstream versions,
but in order to comply with the LGPL-2.1 (§6(a)), we have attached the
complete source code for those libraries which (when combined with the attached
runc source code) may be used to exercise your rights under the LGPL-2.1.

However we strongly suggest that you make use of your distribution's packages
or download them from the authoritative upstream sources, especially since
these libraries are related to the security of your containers.

<hr>

</details>
